Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to analyze each case against the normative process The normative description process starts with Receive order then Check inventory If inventory sufficient Authorize payment then Package items then Ship order then Close order If insufficient Cancel order then Close order Key constraints Authorize payment must occur after Check inventory and before packaging Package items must occur after authorization and before shipping Ship order must occur after packaging and never after cancellation Close order final Also no cancellation after fulfillment activities The description says after cancellation it would be illogical to continue with packaging or shipping so cancellation should be followed directly by close order Also Check inventory must occur before any decisions about payment or fulfillment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1 Receive Check Authorize Package Ship Close This matches the sufficient inventory path perfectly No violation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2 Receive Check Package Authorize Ship Close Note Package items occurs before Authorize payment The normative says Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping Here packaging is before authorization That violates the order authorization must come before packaging Also Authorize payment should only occur after Check inventory and before packaging here authorize occurs after packaging so its after packaging not before So violation activities out of sequence Also the process assumes that if inventory sufficient the sequence is as described But here they packaged before authorization which is not allowed So case 2 violates

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 3 Receive Check Authorize Cancel Ship Close After Check inventory they did Authorize payment Then Cancel order Then Ship order Then Close Normative If inventory insufficient after Check should go directly to Cancel But here they authorized payment first which suggests they thought inventory sufficient But then canceled The description says if inventory insufficient after check proceed to Cancel It doesnt explicitly forbid authorizing payment before cancellation But it says if inventory is found to be sufficient then authorize payment So if its insufficient you should not authorize payment Authorizing payment when inventory insufficient is a deviation Also after cancellation they did Ship order The description Once an order is canceled it would be illogical to continue with activities intended for fulfillment such as packaging or shipping So shipping after cancellation is a violation Also they never packaged items but they shipped Thats odd So case 3 has multiple violations authorizing payment after inventory check but then cancel presumably inventory was insufficient and then shipping after cancellation Also the sequence after cancel should be close not ship So violation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 4 Receive Check Cancel Close This matches the insufficient inventory path perfectly No violation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 5 Receive Check Authorize Ship Close Missing Package items The normative says after authorization should package items then ship Here they skipped packaging and went straight to ship That violates Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping And Ship order should only occur after packaging So missing packaging Also close order is final but the sequence is incomplete So violation

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus cases 2 3 and 5 violate Case 1 and 4 are correct Now explain each violation referencing the description
